subject: FW: Midnight Express
body:
Jeffrey,

 

I asked Larry to drop in on Midnight Express and look at the work being
done.

His thoughts are listed below.

I spoke with Harris of Midnight Express and he feels the Air Conditioner
will be sufficient and the additional weight will not be that big of a
factor.

I guess we will see what happens when we do the sea trial and I would
recommend Carlos fly to Florida to do that.

 

Thank you

 

From: LVJET@aol.com [mailto:LVJET@aol.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, May 13, 2008 11:09 PM
To: Emad Hanna
Cc: DKIESQ@aol.com; lancecalloway@gmail.com; ZorroRanch (AOL)
Subject: Midnight Express

 

 

Emad,

Here are the photos I took today of Midnight Express,

I would recommend a new paint job for boat AFTER a "satisfactory" sea
trial in Florida, the weight increase of the tempered glass is
tremendous.

I am very concerned that the additional weight of 1000 pounds for Glass
and 600 pounds for hard top will make this boat very top heavy and
handle poor, with such a High C.G.

(Center of Gravity). 

I think a lot of attention should be placed on the sea trial before
sending the boat to LSJ... especially sea trial in 4 to 6 foot chop
seas...According to Ron and Eric at Midnight Express this glass
enclosure will be VERY HOT, due to a small air conditioner being
installed, and no free air circulation to cool passenger compartment. 

The limiting factor on Air Conditioner size is the fact of running it
with power from outboard Verado engines,,,,a small generator installed
is most common to run larger air conditioners that can be run at
dockside, while main engines are not running to cool compartment.

 

reminder: this is a 1985 hull.

 

I would fwd to Je for his thoughts.

 

Larry V.

 

 

h c 

 

 

 

 



 

 

 



 

 

 



 

 

 



 

 

 



 





________________________________

Jeffrey,

 

I asked Larry to drop in on Midnight Express and look at the work being done.

His thoughts are listed below.

I spoke with Harris of Midnight Express and he feels the Air Conditioner will be sufficient and the additional weight will not be that big of a factor.

I guess we will see what happens when we do the sea trial and I would recommend Carlos fly to Florida to do that.

 

Thank you

To: Emad Hanna
Cc: DKIESQ@aol.com; lancecalloway@gmail.com; ZorroRanch (AOL)
Subject: Midnight Express

 

 

Emad,

Here are the photos I took today of Midnight Express,

I would recommend a new paint job for boat AFTER a "satisfactory" sea trial in Florida, the weight increase of the tempered glass is tremendous.

I am very concerned that the additional weight of 1000 pounds for Glass and 600 pounds for hard top will make this boat very top heavy and handle poor, with such a High C.G.

(Center of Gravity).

I think a lot of attention should be placed on the sea trial before sending the boat to LSJ... especially sea trial in 4 to 6 foot chop seas...According to Ron and Eric at Midnight Express this glass enclosure will be VERY HOT, due to a small air conditioner being installed, and no free air circulation to cool passenger compartment. 

The limiting factor on Air Conditioner size is the fact of running it with power from outboard Verado engines,,,,a small generator installed is most common to run larger air conditioners that can be run at dockside, while main engines are not running to cool compartment.

 

reminder: this is a 1985 hull.

 

I would fwd to Je for his thoughts.

 

Larry V.
